name: Michael Mayer
headline: Raiders tight end shows pulse but caps low
body:
Mayer posted the best counting season of his career in 2025, yet it still landed him at TE35. Over 13 games on a 61.8 percent snap share he ran 249 routes, drew a 10.1 percent target share and a strong 0.201 targets-per-route rate, catching 35 of 50 targets for 328 yards and a touchdown with just one drop. The problem is depth of usage: a thin 4.8 air yards per target, a -0.197 EPA per play and a 46 percent success rate reveal a checkdown role, even as his 5.6 yards after the catch per reception added juice.

The trend is up from 2024s 15.6 points but still shy of his 2023 rookie pace (44.4 points, 0.418 EPA). A 2023 second-round Notre Dame pick, he profiles as a blocking-first complement whose fantasy value is TD-dependent and matchup-driven. With 6 red-zone targets hinting at scoring upside but no volume foundation, Mayer is a streaming-only tight end and waiver option, not a player to roster through quiet weeks.
